title: MailerLite vs ActiveCampaign (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite and ActiveCampaign: pricing, features, automation, and CRM capabilities to find the best email marketing tool for your business.
intro: MailerLite and ActiveCampaign are two popular email marketing platforms with very different approaches. MailerLite emphasizes simplicity and affordability with a freemium model, while ActiveCampaign focuses on advanced automation and integrated CRM for growing businesses. This comparison will help you decide which platform best fits your marketing needs and budget.
choose_a_if: You're a small business or freelancer with a limited budget and want a free option to start
You need built-in landing pages, pop-ups, and surveys without additional tools
E-commerce features and integrations are important to your business
You prefer simplicity and ease of use over advanced features
choose_b_if: You need advanced automation and lead scoring capabilities for complex workflows
An integrated CRM is essential to manage your customer relationships
You want SMS marketing alongside email campaigns
You require dynamic content and site tracking for personalization
You're willing to invest more for enterprise-level features

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. MailerLite wins for simplicity, affordability, and e-commerce features, making it ideal for small businesses. ActiveCampaign is better for companies needing advanced automation, CRM, and sophisticated marketing workflows. Choose based on your budget and feature requirements.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: MailerLite is significantly cheaper, offering a free tier for up to 1,000 subscribers and paid plans starting at $9/month. ActiveCampaign has no free tier and starts at $15/month. For budget-conscious businesses, MailerLite is the clear winner.

faq.q: Can I switch from ActiveCampaign to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support data exports and integrations with migration tools. However, some advanced ActiveCampaign features (like complex CRM workflows) may not have direct equivalents in MailerLite. Plan your migration carefully to ensure you retain essential functionality.
